title: ficus subincisa var. paucidentata
intro: Ficus subincisa var. paucidentata, commonly known as the Banyan fig, is a deciduous tree species native to Southeast Asia and China, characterized by its small leaves and short, stout branches. The medicinal properties of Ficus subincisa var. paucidentata include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, relieving headaches and pain, and promoting wound healing, as well as supporting cardiovascular and digestive health. Ficus subincisa var. paucidentata can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs, to harness its medicinal and health-promoting properties.
